hq: 166 Geary St. 15th Floor, Suite 145, San Francisco, California 94108, United States

system_description.overview: S2 is a serverless API for durable streams of records, backed by object storage. It is a cloud-based streaming data service that provides control plane APIs for managing basins (containers for streams), streams of records, and access tokens, along with data plane APIs for appending and reading records. The company also provides open source SDKs and integrations.

products.S2 Cloud - Control Plane: API for managing basins, streams, and access tokens with fine-grained scopes
products.S2 Cloud - Data Plane: API for appending records to a stream, reading records from a stream, and checking the last record on a stream
products.S2 Web Dashboard: User management, resource operations on basins/streams/access tokens, usage monitoring and billing

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out method - AWS controls excluded from scope)
regions: Not explicitly named; single region implied from network diagram
infrastructure.physical_presence: None - no physical offices or servers; all cloud-based
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (3 availability zones visible in network diagram: AZ-1, AZ-2, AZ-3)
serverless: Described as 'serverless API' but underlying compute uses container orchestration with Auto Scaling Groups

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Production VPC with segmented VPCs and ACLs. Each of 3 availability zones has private subnets (container orchestration platform with Auto Scaling Groups and worker/validator nodes) and public subnets (NLB interfaces). NAT Gateways per AZ for outbound traffic.
network_architecture.api_gateway: Application gateway mentioned in firewall context (CC6.6.1)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management. Formal network diagram outlining boundary protection mechanisms maintained.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access through bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS encryption for all web-based user requests using certificates from established third-party CA
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server for all systems restricted to internet access
network_architecture.topology: External clients -> Network Load Balancer -> Public Subnets (NLB interfaces in 3 AZs) -> Private Subnets (Container Orchestration Platform with Auto Scaling Groups of worker/validator nodes in 3 AZs) -> Object Storage (S3). NAT Gateways per AZ for outbound. Route 53 public zone for DNS. Internet Gateway at VPC edge.

application_architecture.pattern: Hybrid - serverless API backed by container orchestration platform with auto-scaling worker/validator nodes
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments (CC8.1.4 confirms changes developed and tested in separate environment before implementation). Stage and Production mentioned.

databases: Object storage (S3 - visible in network diagram)
file_storage: S3 (object storage backing the streaming service)
data_storage.backup_frequency: Not explicitly stated; formal backup policies and procedures exist per Backup Policy

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS Password Policy for internal; access tokens with fine-grained scopes for API consumers
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for administrative access to production platform (CC6.1.1, CC6.6); MFA for source code tool access (CC8.1.3); two-factor authentication required for employee email access
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination (CC6.1.4)
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through AWS Password Policy; includes periodic forced changes, password expiry, and complexity requirements. Accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts. Guest and anonymous logins not allowed. Desktops locked after inactivity.

encryption.in_transit: TLS (version not specified) using certificates from established third-party CA
encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (AWS KMS implied - CC6.1.5)
encryption.key_management: Cloud provider key management service for encryption keys; access to production environment access keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Source code management tool (name not disclosed); changes logged, time-stamped, attributed to author (CC8.1.3)
ci_cd_devops.ci_cd_tool: Not named; internal tool tracks changes from initiation through deployment and validation
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to production requires additional approval (CC8.1.2)
ci_cd_devops.iac: Infrastructure as code implied - baseline configurations retained within infrastructure as a code, configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback (CC7.1.1)
ci_cd_devops.container_orchestration: Container Orchestration Platform (visible in network diagram - appears to be ECS or similar with Auto Scaling Groups)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management with monthly patch management reviews by CTO. Workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned for patch compliance daily. OS patches tested for stability and availability before production deployment.

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Monitoring tools (not named) for system capacity and performance monitoring
monitoring_logging.siem: Not named; automated alerts for high and critical risk events
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled at application and infrastructure level for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, security configurations, permissions, and roles (CC7.2.1)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events; incident management process invoked for confirmed events

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure provider - data center services, compute, storage, networking
TLS certificate authority for web encryption
Endpoint security solution for production servers and workstations

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed on all production servers (continuous scan, weekly definition updates) and Mac-based workstations/laptops. Email scanning for malicious scripts and viruses.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; frequency adjusted as required. Also self-assessment scan prior to every release or on yearly basis.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party. Results reviewed by IT management. High/critical findings remediated in timely manner.

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access to facilities: AWS responsible for data center physical security; not in scope
A1.2 - Environmental protections/backup/recovery infrastructure: S2 has no office or working space; managed by building management/AWS
Processing Integrity - Determined not relevant to the system
Privacy - Determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC8.1.2 - Branch protection requiring merge request approval for production changes
CC8.1.3 - Source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed; MFA required for source code tool access
CC7.1.1 - Infrastructure as code with configuration management tool and rollback capability
CC6.1.1 - MFA enforced for administrative access to production
CC6.1.3 - Customer access appropriately segmented from other customer accounts (tenant isolation)
CC4.1.1 - Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
CC7.4.1 - BCDR plans tested annually per defined testing schedule across different loss scenarios
CC6.6.1 - Firewalls and intrusion detection tools configured on application gateway and production network

infrastructure.rationale: AWS with Multi-AZ (3 AZs), VPC with proper segmentation, NLB, Auto Scaling Groups, and IaC. However, single-region only, no CDN mentioned, no multi-region failover. Solid for a startup but lacks geographic redundancy.

application_architecture.rationale: Container orchestration with auto-scaling worker/validator nodes is good architecture. However, languages, frameworks, and specific compute services are not disclosed. Branch protection and code review are confirmed. API style is REST via HTTP endpoints.

data_layer.rationale: Object storage (S3) is the primary backing store which fits the product's append-only stream model. However, no caching layer, no data warehouse, no database specifics beyond S3. Backup frequency not stated. Data classification and retention policies exist. Per-tenant segregation confirmed.

security_posture.rationale: Strong fundamentals: MFA on production and source code, IDS/IPS, firewalls on app gateway, annual pen testing, monthly vulnerability scanning, endpoint protection, anti-malware, server hardening, content filtering via proxy. Missing: no WAF mentioned, no named SIEM tool, no cybersecurity insurance disclosed.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC with configuration management and rollback capability. Branch protection with merge request approval required. Separate dev/test/stage/production environments. Emergency change process documented. Source code management with MFA. However, no specific CI/CD tool, container orchestration, or source control platform named.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, documented and reviewed annually. Annual testing of BCDR plans across different loss scenarios. Multi-AZ failover with servers spanning all availability zones. However, no multi-region failover, RTO/RPO not disclosed, backup frequency not specified.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Heavy single-vendor dependency on AWS for all infrastructure. No additional cloud providers, no CDN vendor, no named monitoring/SIEM/CI-CD vendors. This represents significant vendor concentration risk.

overall.rationale: S2/Bandar Systems has a solid security foundation for an early-stage company achieving its first SOC 2 Type 1. The Multi-AZ architecture with container orchestration and proper network segmentation is well-designed. Security controls are comprehensive with MFA, IDS/IPS, annual pen testing, and vulnerability scanning. Key gaps include single-region deployment, heavy AWS concentration, minimal technology disclosure (no named tools for most categories), and no stated RTO/RPO. The Type 1 nature means operating effectiveness has not yet been validated.

red_flags: Type 1 report only - no operating effectiveness tested; controls are designed but not proven over a period
Single-region deployment with no multi-region failover creates geographic risk
RTO and RPO not disclosed despite having BCDR policy
Heavy AWS vendor concentration with no secondary cloud provider
No WAF mentioned despite having public-facing HTTP API endpoints
Backup frequency not specified in report despite formal backup policy existing
yellow_flags: Most technology tools not named (CI/CD, source control, monitoring, SIEM, container platform) - limits ability to assess actual tool maturity
No CDN mentioned for a public API service
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria excluded as 'not relevant' - unusual for a data storage/streaming service
No SSO mentioned for internal identity management
Patch management references Windows/Network team which seems inconsistent with a cloud-native serverless company
green_flags: Clean unqualified opinion with zero exceptions
Multi-AZ architecture across 3 availability zones with proper auto-scaling
MFA enforced at multiple layers: production admin, source code, employee email
Infrastructure as code with configuration management and rollback capability
Branch protection requiring merge request approval before production deployment
Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with frequency adjusted as needed
Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including admin-privileged
Formal vendor management program with SOC report reviews and quarterly risk assessments
Network diagram provided showing well-structured VPC architecture with private/public subnet separation
BCDR plans tested annually across different loss scenarios
Access revocation within one business day of termination
key_observations: The product is described as a 'serverless API' but the network diagram reveals container orchestration with EC2-based Auto Scaling Groups - this is not truly serverless but rather a managed container platform
The network diagram shows worker and validator nodes suggesting a distributed consensus or verification architecture for the streaming data service

Report uses very generic/template language in many control descriptions (particularly CC1.x through CC5.x) which are nearly identical to boilerplate SOC 2 reports - suggests audit firm may have used standardized templates
Despite being a data streaming/storage company, the report excludes Processing Integrity criteria, which would be highly relevant to verifying data is processed completely and accurately
The org chart shows only CEO -> CTO/CISO -> Developer team/SRE team, indicating a very small company (likely under 20 employees)
No mention of any specific database technology beyond S3 object storage, which is unusual - the control plane likely requires metadata storage that is not disclosed
The NLB (Network Load Balancer) in the diagram rather than ALB suggests TCP/gRPC-level load balancing rather than HTTP-level, consistent with a low-latency streaming data service

Report was signed August 24, 2025 and digitally signed August 29, 2025 - very quick turnaround from audit date of August 4, suggesting a fast audit engagement typical of Type 1 point-in-time assessments
